data: Ricerche di antichità e di epigrafia siceliote
Giacomo Manganaro, «Ricerche di antichità e di epigrafia siceliote», Archeologia Classica 17 (1965): 183–210
Text incised on the projecting upper and lower lintels/borders of the plaque

Slightly irregular deep letters of classical form, moderately wide modul and terminal serifs. Alpha with straight bar; epsilon with shorter middle bar; kappa with shorter arms; mu with vertical first and last, shorter mid-strokes; omicron slightly smaller and above the line, rho with round closed eye; omega circular and full size.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2018-07-06

Three lines of large Greek letters; all are incomplete, but the unjustified left margin suggests a text that was originally centred on the stone

Thinly incised broad letters of regular height, without serifs or marked terminations to the strokes. Alpha has straight bar; Beta has closed loops, in the second instance separated from one another. Sigma has parallel top and bottom strokes, the mid-strokes meeting slightly over halfway across.
Letters of the 3rd century BCE; the apparent double kingship reference suggests the Hieronian period

4[Συρακόσιοι]
Orsi suggested the text was probably originally centred on the rock, but only the right part of three lines now preserved.

Large letters, rendered somewhat crude and deep due to the coarse stone. Sigma is four-bar with slightly open upper and lower strokes; omega is large, rounded and open with short tails; alpha has straight bar; epsilon has shorter middle bar; nu has three full length strokes, first and last vertical; mu has vertical first and last, but shorter middle strokes descending only half-way; omicron is full-size; rho has fairly large rounded, closed eye.
Later 4th or 3rd century BCE on the basis of the letter forms
Vollgraff, 1951, Inhumation en terre sacrée dans l'antiquité grecque (à propos d'une inscription d'Argos)
Masson, 1986, Vocabulaire grec et épigraphie: ἀρχός "chef, archonte"

Inscription erected by Tettia, priestess of Ceres for her son

Six lines of Latin letters, lines 1 and 3 (the start of each individual name) set hard against the left margin, lines 2, 4-6 consistently indented by approximately half a letter space. The inscription neatly fills the available field.

Clear v-cut letters, relatively short and broad, with modest serifs. Cross-strokes are faint, width of letters somewhat uneven; C, V, M notably broad; M has four sloping, full-length strokes; R is slightly open with extended tail; T has upward sloping / curved horizontal; deep triangular interpuncts.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2026-02-04

Wide, slightly irregular in size v-cut letters: alpha wide with straight bar; epsilon has shorter middle bar; omicron is full size; pi has equal length hastae; omega is rhomboid and almost closed; sigma has parallel top and bottom strokes, and slight tendency to curvature in line 2.

Two lines of Greek letters across the upper part of the face; the text fills the width of the stone on both lines

Somewhat crude, large letters, probably later Hellenistic: sigma has parallel top and bottom strokes; omega is closed, tending to elliptical in form; mu has vertical first and last strokes, mid-point descends half-way only; phi has small circular eye and extended vertical; kappa has short tails; alpha has broken bar; epsilon has short middle stroke

1Augusto

The text is engraved on the upper surface of the stone (a second text on the front face); a large circular hole intrudes into the first visible line of the text, and the stone is broken away on the right side of the upper part, so the original extent of the text is unclear, but there appears to be a vacat after the third visible line

Plain letters of the middle Hellenistic period: Pi has verticals of equal length; sigma has parallel top and bottom strokes; omicron is small and mid-line; epsilon has a short middle stroke; mu has vertical first and last strokes, while the middle point only descends part way; alpha has both broken and straight bar
Photo J. Prag, courtesy Museo della cultura e delle Arti Figurative Bizantine e Normanne
Cordiano, 1997, La ginnasiarchia nelle "poleis" dell'occidente mediterraneo antico
Manganaro, 1999, Sikelika: studi di antichità e di epigrafia della Sicilia greca

Text is engraved in the upper left corner of the front face of the stele; the text is incomplete on the right sides as the right upper corner is lost; however there is clear vacat below.

Letters of the mid-Hellenistic period: epsilon has short middle bar; omicron is small and mid-line; mu has vertical first and last strokes, the mid-point descends half-way; alpha is formed with both broken and straight bar; sigma has approximately parallel top and bottom strokes; omega is open; theta formed with a central point.

1Ὁ δᾶ[μος ---]
Four lines of Greek letters approximately centred on the upper half of the front face.

Simply cut, regular letters of fairly wide module, without serifs. Alpha with broken bar; epsilon with shorter middle stroke; Mu with full length strokes, vertical first and last; omicron almost full size; sigma with horizontal outer strokes, inner strokes almost full width; phi with extended vertical (up to 65mm), small circular eye; omega closed, with tear-drop shape.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2022/07/14

Simple deep cut letters, omicron slightly smaller than other letters, phi extended to 5 cm.

1[--- ]υ̣λλος̣ [---]
2[--- ]ιφιλ[--- ---]
The text is laid out in a single column, with a consistent left margin, with every second line indented by c.1 letter; the right margin is irregular.

Letters are plainly cut with minimal serifs and a square module. Alpha has straight bar; beta has equal closed loops; epsilon sometimes has equal length strokes, sometimes shorter middle bar; theta is full size, slightly elliptical, with plain cross-bar; kappa has full length arms; mu has vertical first and last, with middle strokes only half-descending; pi has overlapping horizontal, equal length vertical strokes; rho has closed eye of moderate size; sigma has horizontal upper and lower strokes; phi has an ovoid eye of varying size and extended vertical; omega varies between teardrop, rhomboid and small circle, always closed with horizontal baseline.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2023-05-10.

Large, regular letters of generally fairly broad module, but with some irregularlity in the width of the module. Lunate epsilon and sigma, uncial omega. Large round omicron. narrow alpha with broken bar.
